title: Adaptive Network Control (ANC) Action Auditing and Excessive Quarantine Detection
dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Adaptive Network Control* category — codes `60150` (ANC policy applied to endpoint), `60151` (ANC policy removed from endpoint), `60152` (ANC quarantine — auto), `60153` (ANC quarantine — manual via UI), `60154` (ANC quarantine — pxGrid published action), `60155` (ANC unquarantine), `60156` (ANC port-bounce executed), `60157` (ANC port-shutdown executed). Fields: `endpoint_mac`, `anc_policy`, `triggered_by` (system|admin|pxgrid|api), `admin_user`, `policy_name`. CIM mapping: `Change.action=modified`, `Change.object_category=anc_quarantine`.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (60150, 60151, 60152, 60153, 60154, 60155, 60156, 60157)
| rex "endpoint_mac=(?<mac>[^,\s]+)"
| rex "anc_policy=(?<policy>[^,\s]+)"
| rex "triggered_by=(?<trigger>[^,\s]+)"
| rex "admin_user=(?<admin>[^,\s]+)"
| eval action = case(
    MessageCode IN (60150,60152,60153,60154,60156,60157), "quarantine",
    MessageCode IN (60151, 60155), "unquarantine")
| stats count by trigger, action, admin
| eventstats sum(eval(if(action=="quarantine",count,0))) as total_q sum(eval(if(action=="unquarantine",count,0))) as total_unq
| eval ratio = round(total_q / (total_unq+1), 2)
| where (action=="quarantine" AND count > 50) OR (trigger=="admin" AND count > 10)
description: Audits all ANC actions (quarantine, unquarantine, port-bounce, port-shutdown) and detects excessive quarantine activity that may signal a misconfigured automation, a compromised admin account, or a legitimate large-scale incident-response action.
value: ANC is the live arm of incident response — when SOAR/SIEM/SOC pushes a quarantine action, it's enforced through ANC. Auditing every action is foundational for incident-response evidence, compliance reporting, and detecting misuse (compromised admin pushing thousands of quarantine actions). Excessive quarantine detection is also a 'kill-switch' — if a runaway automation is quarantining the entire fleet, this UC catches it within 5 minutes.
implementation: Confirm the *Adaptive Network Control* category is at INFO. Schedule the search every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `trigger`+`admin` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single value: ANC actions last 24h. (2) Stacked bar: actions by trigger source last 7d. (3) Top-N: admin users by ANC actions last 30d (audit). (4) Trend: quarantine vs unquarantine ratio over 30d.
dataModelAcceleration: Yes — Change DM acceleration helps long-window audit reports.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **Phishing-tabletop drill** — A planned IR exercise generates many quarantine actions in a short window. Cross-reference change tickets / IR exercise calendar. (b) **SOAR playbook bulk-action** — A SOAR playbook quarantining endpoints based on a campaign rule may legitimately push 50+ actions. Treat as expected if from a known SOAR service account. (c) **Initial deployment of an automated rule** — First-day operation may quarantine many endpoints as the ruleset evaluates against the fleet. Suppress for 24h after a known new rule deployment.
mitreAttack: N/A (control-plane response — Adaptive Network Control quarantine telemetry, not adversary behaviour)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(b) requires incident handling. ANC is the enforcement arm of incident response; auditing evidences the operational fidelity of incident response.
compliance.controlObjective: Operational fidelity and audit of incident-response enforcement actions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.42 alert log + monthly ANC audit report retained 7y.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.17 (ICT-related incident management process) — ANC is incident-response enforcement; audit evidences fidelity.
IR-4 (Incident Handling) requires implementation of incident handling capability. ANC monitoring evidences the enforcement arm of incident handling.
AU-6 (Audit Record Review and Analysis) — ANC actions are audit records that require review.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.42 audit log + monthly review attestation.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.5.26 (Response to information security incidents) — ANC is the response arm; audit evidences operational integrity.
CC7.4 (Common Criteria — Response to Identified Issues) — ANC is the response action; audit evidences fidelity.

controlTest.positiveScenario: From the ISE PAN UI, select an endpoint and apply an ANC policy. ISE emits 60150/60153 events. UC-17.1.42 records the action. Repeat 11 times in 15 min and the alert fires for the admin user.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Apply 5 ANC actions in 15 min via SOAR (with a service account flagged as low-noise). UC-17.1.42 records the actions but does not alert (count threshold not crossed).

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE *Adaptive Network Control* category at INFO.
• `anc_authorized_admins.csv` lookup of admin users authorised to apply ANC actions manually.
• Splunk role with `srchIndexesAllowed = nac`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate event flow:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (60150, 60153) earliest=-7d
| stats count by trigger, admin_user
```

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as in controlTest. Confirm alert.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — ANC Audit":
• Single value: actions last 24h, last 30d.
• Stacked bar: actions by trigger.
• Top-N: admins by action volume.
• Trend: quarantine/unquarantine ratio.

Monthly audit attestation: ANC admin team signs off on the report; archived 7 years.

Alerting: high-urgency on excessive quarantine; medium on unauthorised admin (admin_user not in lookup).

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **`admin_user` blank** — Action triggered by automation/pxGrid not by a human admin; expected.
• **Excessive volume from one SOAR account** — Coordinate with the SOAR team to investigate the playbook.
• **No 60150 events** — *Adaptive Network Control* category not enabled or set to WARN.
grandmaExplanation: When ISE blocks or releases a device — like putting it in time-out — we record exactly who or what made the decision. If too many time-outs happen too quickly, we ring the bell so a human can verify it's not a runaway automation or a misuse of admin powers.
